mnemonics.title: Phong Components
mnemonics.content: Phong = P-A-D-S (Phong = Purely Ambient Diffuse Specular) - Remember: P-A-D-S order matches formula: I = ka*Ia + kd*Ii*(N·L) + ks*Ii*(R·V)^n

mnemonics.title: Light Types Memory
mnemonics.content: P-D-S-A: Point (position), Directional (direction), Spot (cone), Ambient (all directions equally)

mnemonics.title: Shading Quality Order
mnemonics.content: FGP: Flat (Fastest/First), Gouraud (Good), Phong (Premium/Finest) - in increasing quality order

acronyms.usage: The three components of the Phong illumination model
Cosine of angle between surface normal and light direction for diffuse intensity
Specular intensity calculation - higher value means view aligns with reflection
Used in Blinn-Phong: H = normalize(L + V) - simplifies specular calculation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a lightbulb at a point in space - rays emanate equally in all directions like rays from the sun but from a finite point
Think of a flashlight or stage spotlight - a cone of light with bright center and darker edges, limited to a specific direction
Imagine parallel rays of sunlight - all rays come from the same direction, no matter where you are in the scene
The shiny white spot you see on a polished sphere when light hits it - its size and sharpness indicate surface smoothness

analogies.analogy: Like the overall brightness in a room with curtains drawn - not direct light but enough to see by
Like matte paint on a wall - light bounces off in all directions equally, no shiny spots
Like a mirror or polished metal - light bounces off at a specific angle creating clear reflections
Like sound getting quieter as you move away from a speaker - intensity decreases with distance
